title: Markov Staircases
speaker: Joé Brendel
abstract: In this talk, we will discuss new infinite symplectic staircases. Much recent progress has been made in the study of infinite symplectic staircases arising from embedding problems of standard ellipsoids into various symplectic four-manifolds. We study new embedding problems, where the domains are "ellipsoid-like" neighbourhoods of Lagrangian pinwheels instead of standard ellipsoids. Using almost toric fibrations, we show that every Lagrangian pinwheel in the complex projective plane (and thus every Markov number) has an infinite symplectic staircase. This is joint work with Jonny Evans, Johannes Hauber and Felix Schlenk.

proof_stages.label: Classical ellipsoid-staircase framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls the CP2 ellipsoid embedding problem, its volume constraint, the MacDuff-Schlenk infinite staircase, the target normalization convention, and the role of almost toric fibrations in construction and obstruction.

proof_stages.label: RP2 ellipsoid embedding problem
proof_stages.summary: RP2 ellipsoids are defined as triangular domains in the almost toric base of T*RP2, giving neighborhoods of the zero section, and the admissible alpha-beta embedding region into CP2 is formulated with its volume bound.

proof_stages.label: RP2 Markov staircase from ATF mutations
proof_stages.summary: Nodal trades and mutations of CP2 almost toric fibrations produce volume-filling RP2 ellipsoid embeddings whose steps are parametrized by Markov triples containing 2, and the theorem states that the resulting staircase is exactly the admissible region in the relevant square.

proof_stages.label: General (p,q)-ellipsoids and pinwheel admissibility
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines B_{p,q} by an almost toric cone with a branch cut, identifies the corresponding ellipsoids as smooth neighborhoods of Lagrangian pinwheels, and recalls that Evans-Smith admissibility requires p to be a Markov number with q its companion.

proof_stages.label: Lambda_pq staircase theorem
proof_stages.summary: For each admissible Markov p, a lambda-shaped subtree of Markov triples preserving p gives two branches of volume-filling points, and the main theorem says this possibly asymmetric Markov staircase is the full admissible set, not just a construction.

proof_stages.label: Reduction to ray obstructions
proof_stages.summary: To prove exactness, it suffices to obstruct a slight simultaneous enlargement past a staircase point along a ray, because any larger embedding would contain a forbidden smaller one on that ray, and the planned contradiction comes from cutting to a space with a negative-area holomorphic curve.

proof_stages.label: Pseudo-rational blowup and visible -1 sphere apparatus
proof_stages.summary: Rational blowup is reviewed through ATF base surgery, then a modified cut parallel to the obstructing node's branch cut produces a family of smooth symplectic manifolds with a visible embedded -1 sphere of area T-t.

proof_stages.label: Gromov-Witten invariant contradiction
proof_stages.summary: Using uniqueness of small pinwheel neighborhoods, the standard pseudo-blowup family is compared to the one coming from a hypothetical enlarged embedding, and deformation invariance of the nonzero invariant for the -1 class would force a holomorphic curve with negative area, giving the contradiction.
